steps.title: Identify the angle relationship at the intersection
steps.content: Two straight lines intersect at a single point, creating four angles. Angles $r$ and $s$ are positioned opposite each other at the intersection—they are vertically opposite angles (also called vertical angles).

steps.title: Apply the vertical angles theorem
steps.content:
When two lines intersect, vertically opposite angles are always equal. Since $r$ and $s$ are vertical angles and $r = 61°$, we have $s = r = 61°$.

That matches choice 61.
